Gyro-Trac Marketing · June 2026 Plan

Our June Plan ·
The Chuck Leavell Launch
+ Refurb GT-25 Push

A full-month plan. Chuck's launch carries the awareness side; the two refurbished GT-25 dedicated mulchers are the hard sales push. Three emails, Dealer of the Month, Tim-led video, and July 4th prep.

Launch · June 213-min Interview · 8 ShortsEmail · 3 Sends to 10KRefurb GT-25 · Captive + Steel TrackCutter Heads · Supporting Social
1

Where We Stand

May 19 Sales · May 27 Marketing

Closed Out · May 19 Sales Meeting

Lead list — sentMarketing leads organized and handed to sales for follow-up.
Customer list — sentUpdated customer contacts delivered to the team for outreach.
Interview footage — chopped & editedAll assets in the Google Drive 2026 folder. 17 shorts cut. Final interview edit is 13 minutes.
Campaign data — reviewedPrior campaign: 140 opens / 81 clicks. Strongest engagement in SC + the Southeast.

Aligned · May 27 Marketing Meeting

June pivot · GT-25 over chipperRefurbished GT-25 captive + steel track mulchers are the June sales engine. Chipper takes a back seat.
Blog is the traffic hubAll social and email funnels route to the blog landing page — not directly to YouTube.
Launch date · June 2Pushed one day from June 1 to allow Chuck's team final approval. Blog + YouTube go live Tuesday morning.
Email · 10,000-contact listThree sends planned across June: Chuck legacy, follow-up to openers, then the GT-25 refurb hard push.
Dealer of the Month series · adoptedRecurring content to strengthen partners + create FOMO for new dealer interest. Tim selects who's first.
Tim-led professional video · adoptedSales correlate with Tim on camera. Coordinate a real produced shoot.
Trade show · canceledConfirmed by Patricia: the event was the DOT RoadEO (invitation only). No availability to attend. No flyers needed.
July 4th campaign · parts-focused brainstormPlanning kicks off in W3 so creative is ready before the holiday.

In Progress · Moving

Refurb serial numbers — received from OlivierWendy + Ryan are working on adding both GT-25 units to the app and website now. Targeting live by June 16 so they anchor the W3 push.
Ripping It Outdoors research — completeRyan investigated the site, the brand portfolio, federal registration, and reviews. Full findings + recommendation in Section 9 below.

Still Awaiting

Chuck pre-approval — Daniel sending unlisted linkHard blocker for the Tuesday send. If approval doesn't land in time, Email 1 slides to Friday June 5.
2

The Story We're Telling

Why this lands
“It's my
wife's fault.”

The hook isn't equipment specs — it's a Rolling Stones keyboardist who's also a National Outstanding Tree Farmer of the Year. A 53-year marriage, a family heritage of land stewardship, and the idea that "instruments come from wood, so I give back to the forest." That emotional spine carries the whole launch. The machine is the proof, not the pitch.

3

The Launch Rollout

3 Phases
June 2
AM

Phase 1 · Blog — The Traffic Hub

This is where every funnel points. Social + email all route here. Three-beat story (the unlikely origin, the stewardship philosophy, the GT-25 delivery), the YouTube video embedded inside, lead-capture form, and CTAs to the refurb GT-25 mulchers and cutter heads. Drafting next once approved.

June 2
AM

Phase 2 · YouTube — Where the Video Lives

The 13-minute interview hosted publicly so the blog can embed it. Title: "Why a Rolling Stones Legend Bought a Gyro-Trac." Thumbnail: Chuck + Daniel by the machine. Description points back to the blog — every comment pin links to the blog, not the YouTube channel.

June
3–10

Phase 3 · Social Shorts — The Drip

We don't dump all 17. 8 hand-picked clips drip out daily, strongest first. Every CTA links to the blog — that's the conversion point, not YouTube. See the lineup below.

4

The 8 Shorts We Run

From 17 → 8
DateClipWhy it makes the cut
June 3Chuck Leavell — "Music legend, forestry guru"The scroll-stopper. Identity reveal — strongest possible opener.
June 4From Music to Land StewardThe emotional "why." Highly shareable.
June 5Trees Grow From Air, Not SoilSurprising fact — drives comments and shares.
June 6Award-Winning Tree FarmerCredibility punch.
June 7Low-Impact Fire Clears BrushFirst real product-benefit clip.
June 8Mulching MachinesProduct education — natural bridge into the GT-25 refurb push.
June 9Wood: Heart of Your Favorite InstrumentsPoetic — ties the whole story together.
June 10Turn Waste Into WealthBusiness / ROI angle for buyers — sets up the sales push.
Hold as B-library:  Quail Hunting · Kubota Skid Steer · Meet Gabe · 42 Years Strong · Introducing My Wife · "WE" · America's Forests TV · From Musician to Land. Good clips — just niche or redundant with stronger picks. Save them for Email 2 follow-up or filler weeks.
5

How Each Platform Handles It

The Blog

The destination. Where every funnel terminates. Lead capture lives here. Embedded video, story copy, and CTAs to the refurb GT-25 mulchers and cutter heads.

YouTube

Hosts the 13-minute interview, posted June 2 AM. Description + pinned comment both link back to the blog.

Facebook / Instagram

Reels, vertical, captions burned in for sound-off viewing. IG also gets 2–3 still pulls as feed posts. All CTAs link to the blog.

LinkedIn

Where this over-performs. B2B framing — "what a music legend taught us about land stewardship." 3 strongest clips spaced across 10 days. Our dealer + government buyers live here.

Before anything goes public: Chuck's team reviews the material first. Daniel is sending the unlisted YouTube link. Approval has to land before Tuesday morning or Email 1 slides to Friday June 5. This is the one true blocker.

6

The June Calendar — Story + Sales

Two threads, one month

The June priority is moving the two refurbished GT-25 dedicated mulchers. Chuck's launch carries the awareness side, and the GT-25 refurb units are the hard sales push. Cutter heads and replacement teeth ride along as supporting social content, not the headline.

W1Launch

StoryChuck launch owns the week · June 2–8

YouTube + blog drop Tuesday June 2 (or Friday June 5 if Chuck's team needs more time). Email 1 sends same morning to the full 10K list. Shorts drip Days 3–8. Sales content stays quiet — we don't split attention while the launch is finding its audience. Tim-video shoot scheduled this week if possible.

W2Bridge

StorySalesEmail 2 follow-up + GT-25 refurb tease · June 9–15

Final Chuck shorts wrap. Email 2 sends ~7 days after Email 1 — a follow-up to Email 1 openers, deepening the story and softly bridging to the GT-25 refurb lineup. Refurb listings should go live this week. First Dealer of the Month post. Cutter head / teeth supporting posts begin.

W3Push

SalesRefurb GT-25 hard push + Email 3 · June 16–22

This is the sales week. The two refurb GT-25 mulchers (captive + steel track) carry the hero content — demo clips, specs, "ready to work" angles. Email 3 sends ~June 15 (or later in W3) with the hard refurb GT-25 pitch. Cutter heads + teeth keep rolling as supporting social. Soft ADPRO social proof (confirm with Tim). July 4th creative brainstorm kicks off this week.

W4Convert

StorySalesTie story to sale, convert, set up July · June 23–30

Bring it together: the stewardship story Chuck told + the iron that does the work. CTA-heavy posts — claim a refurb GT-25, quote a head, order teeth, book a demo. Targets the warm list built across the month. July 4th parts campaign creative finalized for early-July send.

7

On Deck — Two Refurbished GT-25 Dedicated Mulchers

The priority sale of June

Two dedicated GT-25 mulcher units are the priority sale of June, coming to market through the refurbished equipment channel. Factory photography isn't ready yet, so we start with refurb imagery and route all traffic to the refurbished equipment section. Once live, they're the centerpiece of W3 and Email 3.

GT-25 · Captive Track

Dedicated mulcher unit. Captive track configuration. Listing on the refurb page. Specs, photos (refurb imagery as placeholder), and CTA.

GT-25 · Steel Track

Dedicated mulcher unit. Steel track configuration. Listing on the refurb page. Specs, photos (refurb imagery as placeholder), and CTA.

Status: Olivier delivered the refurb serial numbers ✓. Wendy + Ryan are actively adding both units to the app and website — targeting live by June 16 to anchor the W3 hard push. Once live: hero social, Email 3, and the conversion week (W4) all hit these units.

8

Other June Initiatives

From the May 27 marketing meeting

Dealer of the Month

Recurring content series across social + email. Strengthens current partners and creates FOMO that pulls new dealer interest. Tim picks who's first.

Tim-Led Pro Video

Sales correlate with Tim on camera. Coordinate a real produced session — better lighting, better setup, scripted talking points. Runs alongside the ongoing phone-recorded content. Ryan + Tim to schedule.

DOT RoadEO · Canceled

The "Columbia SC rodeo" was the DOT RoadEO — invitation-only event for the Department of Transportation. Per Patricia, we had no availability to attend. No flyers needed. Worth tracking for future years if invited.

July 4th · Parts-Focused

Brainstorm in W3, creative built in W4, send right before the holiday. Parts angle: replacement teeth + the "keep your iron running this summer" pitch. Ties cleanly into the cutter head supporting social.

9

Ripping It Outdoors — Research Findings

Ryan · May 27 action item
Verdict ·
Worth a call.
Not a commitment.

Legitimate business, real federal contractor, multi-brand catalog — but they already sell our competitors (Barko, CMI, Lamtrac) and look more like a Shopify aggregator than a forestry specialist. A conversation with their Programs team is worth the time; a signed dealer agreement is not, until we vet the structure.

The Facts

  • Business: Ripping It Outdoors LLC. Miami FL HQ at 2125 Biscayne Blvd. Shopify-powered store at rippingitoutdoors.com. Phone (305) 391-2008. Tim said warehouse in Jupiter FL — that doesn't match what's online. Worth verifying.
  • Federal contractor: Registered (UEI EYBJLAQBFDQ8, CAGE 9UNB2). Real credential — opens gov / university / municipal procurement channels.
  • Brand portfolio: 20+ brands across forestry, ag, marine, drones, e-bikes, even yacht water purifiers. Forestry overlap with us: Barko, CMI, Lamtrac, ASV, Kanga, Tobroco GiANT, Develon.
  • Service claims: "Nationwide service," financing partners, "Machine Match Maker" tool that pairs attachments to customer machines, government procurement, even outdoor hunting/fishing trips.
  • Reputation signals: 88% recommend on Facebook (only 19 reviews — small sample), LinkedIn presence, active YouTube / Instagram / TikTok. ProvenExpert profile only created Oct 2024.
  • "Seven generations of family enterprise" claim on the About page — bold and unverifiable. Treat as marketing copy until proven.

What They Could Bring

National e-commerce reach. Existing customer base shopping for forestry mulchers (they already sell Barko / CMI / Lamtrac). Federal contractor status unlocks gov procurement. Financing channel partners. "Machine Match Maker" could route their skid-steer customers to our cutter heads.

What to Watch For

They sell our direct competitors — any deal would be non-exclusive. Catalog breadth (yacht filters to drones to mulchers) suggests aggregator / drop-ship model, not a forestry specialist. Likely no demo machines, no in-house service techs for forestry equipment. The Jupiter FL warehouse claim doesn't appear online.

Recommended next step: Set up a discovery call with their Programs team. Five questions to answer before signing anything: (1) Real warehouse + service techs or drop-ship? (2) Authorized dealer or independent reseller for CMI / Barko? (3) What's the exclusivity / margin / training structure? (4) Can they provide 2–3 current dealer references? (5) Could they be a parts / teeth distribution channel even if not a whole-machine partner?

10

June Email — Three Sends

Story → Re-engage → Sell
Tues June 2 · or Fri June 5 latest

Email 1 · The Chuck "Legacy" Send

"A Rolling Stones legend just bought a Gyro-Trac"
  • The Chuck interview — story-led, low pressure.
  • CTA links to the blog (which embeds the video).
  • Goes to the full 10,000-contact list — re-warms everyone.
  • Send Tuesday if Chuck approves Monday. Otherwise slides to Friday June 5.
~1 week after Email 1

Email 2 · Follow-up to Openers

"Loved Chuck's story? Here's the rest."
  • Audience: openers of Email 1 only.
  • Deeper Chuck content — behind-the-scenes / B-library short.
  • Soft bridge into the refurb GT-25 lineup arriving on the site.
  • Goal: deepen engagement with the warm audience, prime them for the hard sell coming.
~Mon June 15 · or later in W3

Email 3 · GT-25 Refurb Push

"Two GT-25 mulchers, ready to work."
  • The hard sell. Refurb GT-25 captive + steel track.
  • Specs, photos, urgency — "claim yours" CTA.
  • Cutter heads + teeth as supporting mention (parts where you need them).
  • Segmented: high-intent openers + SC / Southeast leads + Email 2 openers.
  • Requires the refurb listings to be live first.

The sequence: story warms the list (Email 1), re-engagement deepens the warmest audience (Email 2), the GT-25 refurb pitch lands on the hottest list of the month (Email 3). Each send raises the temperature for the next.

What We Need From This Meeting

  1. Green light · Email 1 send timing — sign off on Tuesday June 2 (preferred) or Friday June 5 (backup) so Ryan can queue the send. Decision depends on Chuck's approval landing today / Monday.
  2. Wendy + Ryan · progress check — confirm both refurb GT-25 listings (captive + steel track) will be live on the website by June 16. Email 3 + the W3 hero content depend on it.
  3. Tim — pick the first Dealer of the Month feature, and OK to name ADPRO as social proof in the W3 push?
  4. Ryan + Tim — lock a date for the Tim-led pro video shoot. Earlier in June = more sales lift across the month.
  5. Sales team — any specific cutter head models or teeth SKUs to feature in supporting social posts? We'll prioritize what's moving.
  6. Tim / Group — do we want Ryan to schedule a discovery call with Ripping It Outdoors? Research is in Section 9. Worth a conversation, not a commitment yet.
Gyro-Trac · Forestry & Land Management · June 2026 Marketing Plan
